headlineSummary: HN comments leaned neutral overall, with OpenAI getting one negative note on code maintainability and Anthropic drawing mixed feedback from one praise, two critiques, and a negative aside about the C compiler experiment [E3][E1][E2].

ranking.summary: OpenAI drew a negative comment for Codex: it was praised for debugging, but the produced code was described as really bad and not maintainable [E3].

Anthropic had limited evidence and a mixed-neutral tone: one commenter praised Claude for solving a compatibility tweak, while others said it often creates problems and cited a failed C compiler experiment [E1][E2][E3].

evidence.excerpt: but claude was able to figure out a tweak to get it running and hide some of the features the XT30 is too old for

annotations.rationale: The commenter credits Claude with successfully solving a compatibility problem, indicating mild praise for its coding/helpfulness.

evidence.excerpt: My experience is that Claude starts to make quite a mess in this context, and it'll often cause as many problems as it solves unless you have the technical and domain knowledge to redirect and correct it frequently.

annotations.rationale: The commenter directly criticizes Claude's output quality and reliability in software work, saying it creates many problems.

evidence.excerpt: I've been using Codex in anger for the past 2 months or so (with gpt5.3-codex and then gpt5.4) on projects of different complexity. It is quite good at debugging, but the (non-trivial) code it produces is really bad. And I don't mean bad stylistically, but bad in the sense that Codex clearly won't be able to maintain it for long (which is how Anthropic's C compiler experiment failed)

annotations.rationale: Although the commenter notes Codex is good at debugging, they overall judge its non-trivial code output as 'really bad' and not maintainable, making the net stance negative.
The aside says Anthropic's C compiler experiment 'failed,' which is a clear negative mention, though only incidental to the main point about Codex.
